rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Te Aro, Mount Victoria); city center NZD 2,300/mo (~$1,380 USD); outside ~NZD 1,650 (~$990)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; avg $151 utilities + internet $50 reported; Wellington windy = higher heating

groceries.note: home cooking; Countdown/Pak'nSave; NZD $280-400/mo reported range

diningOut.note: Wellington excellent cafe/restaurant scene; lunch ~NZD 18-22, dinner NZD 30-45

transit.note: Snapper card monthly ~NZD 60-100 ($36-60) depending on zone; Wellington compact, walkable

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; Wellington tech hub; ~NZD 280-400/mo; government/tech sector anchor

misc.note: gym (~NZD 45), toiletries, entertainment, vibrant arts scene, weather gear buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Wellington Southern Hemisphere; medium-term furnished lets fairly stable.

evidence.quote: A comfortable monthly budget in Wellington is NZD $4,180, which includes rent, groceries, transport, utilities, dining out, and entertainment
One-bedroom apartments in the city center rent for NZD $2,300/month; A 1-bedroom apartment in the city centre averages $1,197/month (USD), with rent outside the centre costing approximately $993

evidence.claim: Wellington remote worker monthly spend range
evidence.quote: A single remote worker can expect total monthly expenses of $1,800–$2,400 depending on lifestyle choices, with accommodation consuming roughly 50–60% of total spend
